sentences.text: Hi, Cha!
Oh!
Hi, Chad.
Hello.
How are ya?
Good, how you doing?
I'm doing alright.
I would be doing better if we were still on the weekend, but alas, we are here, so…
I know, I know.
It just… it feels like Monday comes so fast every weekend.
I know.
I… I understand what you're saying.
Yes.
the same way.
I know, I think we all do.
Did you have a good weekend, at the very least?
Yeah, you know, not too bad, didn't do much.
Okay.
By yourself, you do anything?
We moved last weekend, so I'm still trying to just kind of settle in.
So, unfortunately, nothing too exciting to report, but I also am getting to be in an age where I enjoy a non-eventful weekend, so…
Yeah.
Yeah, I can… I can do eventful weekends, you know.
Yes.
Once a month.
Two a month, if I'm feeling crazy.
No, truly.
So, we're aligned on that.
So, yeah, we, I'm in Austin, Texas, so, like, October.
Oh, okay.
Like, a wild month.
Oh, yeah.
ACL and F1, so I am in preservation mode, if you will.
You live, you live downtown?
I'm, like, just south of downtown, like, it's like a 10-minute walk, so I'm like…
Oh, it's not bad.
kind of in the mix, but, like, just far enough away.
Yeah.
Kind of the same way in Detroit.
Okay, okay.
a little bit outside, right downtown, so… Okay!
Yes, I feel like the burbs are calling me slowly but surely, but I'm not… I'm not quite there yet.
Yeah.
So, yes, I… wow.
After thinking about everything that I just said, I very much sound like I'm in my 30s.
It's like…
Yeah.
Oh, we're all getting old.
I know, I know.

It sounds like maybe reputation management is kind of on your radar as well, is that correct?
Yeah, yep.
Okay, cool.
So then, yeah, I definitely want to chat through both of those things, kind of get a better understanding of what y'all are doing currently.
Is Lavina still over there?
Who.
Lavina Girard?
I don't… she might be somewhere, but I… I haven't… I haven't worked with her.
Okay, alright, never.
Have you spoken to her before?
She might be one of our apartment managers or something.
Yeah, okay, I don't know, I… maybe I have my wires crossed, which, you know, I wouldn't put it past me.
We have a lot of apartment complexes, so I can't fully keep track of…
Okay, thank you.
manager, so it's very possible that she's still somewhere here.
Okay, okay, fair.
Alright, great.

Well then, yeah, just have a few questions on the front end, just to get a better idea of your strategy, and then at the end of the call, we can decide if we want to keep the convo going, revisit down the road, what have you.
Do you have any questions for me before I hop in?
No, I don't think so.
Okay, well then, I know that I might have just touched on it with reputation management, but was there anything else in particular that piqued your interest in taking the call today?

No, he just gave me a call on, Friday, so… I know, we've been looking for, like, reputation manager, and obviously we don't have a… I mean, it's me and one other guy that basically do every… all the marketing and everything here, so we have… Okay.
We have, like, a hundred and… probably, like, closer to 200 apartment complexes now.
Okay.
I mean, run, so…
It's impossible to really get to all of them.
Yeah, holy moly.
Okay, so there are two of y'all across 200 complexes, and, do y'all own and manage most of them, or do any third parties?
Yeah, we, so it's like a comp… so I technically work for, like, MHT, that… that's, like, the management side.
And then we have Continental, which is also management.
It's like, MHC is non-profit, Continental is for-profit, and then we have a… we have a construction side of our business.

So, a lot of the… a lot of apartments… we manage a lot of apartments, and then we also own quite a few different… mainly, A lot of it's, like, income-restricted housing, although we're getting more and more into, like, the market rate, housing as well, so…
Okay, okay, got it.
So, with that being said, then, you oversee both MHT and Continental, or you're mostly MHT?
Yeah, we're technically MSG, but we look over both.
Okay, okay, got it.
You get paid by MSD.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Alright, great.
So, a little bit of both, mixed bag there.
And then, when it comes to your Google business profiles, are you doing anything with them?
Do you have them claimed?
Yeah, we… we have most of them claim… there's probably certainly some that we don't, but the ones that we're heavily focused on, we make sure to claim all those.
Okay.
And then in terms of… we'll update some of, like, the posting on some of them, but it's pretty… it's pretty hit or miss when it comes to that.

But yeah, we try… I mean, there's still… there's still a decent… there's probably still a decent amount there that we just… Just because I obviously have to do the call and get the code, and sometimes there's not an answer, and we just don't… It's hard to get… run back and do it again, or remind yourself to do it when there's that many apartments, and we've got a million other things doing.
I totally understand.
Okay, so, yeah, the one… I did take a peek at a few, and it looks like y'all had some floor plans loaded, which is great.
So with that being said, I guess when you are posting and that sort of thing, how do you… how do you choose those properties to kind of focus a little bit more on?
Usually we get, like, a, well.
It's either, like, the newer properties.

That we… we just started working with, like, new constructions, a lot of time there, and then… and then we… we usually get, like, a list, basically, of… like, a watch list of properties that we're trying… that we need to get filled up, and those are usually the ones that we'll focus on for however long.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright, great, so it sounds like for the newer properties, then, like, is… do y'all approach that, kind of, from, like.
like, an SEO standpoint of increasing that SEO with getting the Google Business Profile claimed, or is there any sort of strategy that goes into that beyond just getting the profile up and running?

Yeah, I mean, it's obviously just… the SEO definitely helps in terms of… especially when it's a new property, getting your… getting you to rank, but and just uploading… sometimes… I usually try, especially on the new constructions, as… I usually get, like, monthlies of the updated picture, so we'll keep, like, a rendering, typically, until it starts getting close, and then I'll usually start updating.
The images there, but yeah, we always try to make sure, like, the phone numbers, obviously, the websites, so not every one of our, properties, we'll have a website, we have, like.
Continental Management has, like, most to all of our properties on there, and then certain properties, depending on what it is, gets… we'll make them a specific website.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
All right, got it.

Cause yeah, this… so our Google Business Profile automation tool, yeah, ideally, like, we do need some level, like, a landing page, like a property website landing page to kind of… yeah, I guess, like, drive that website traffic on, but okay, perfect.
And then, as far as your reputation process currently, can you talk to me a little bit about that?
We just, I mean, we just pretty much, as we go, we… I mean, when we get bad reviews, we essentially try to…
Okay.
Get them off any way we can, but it's… I mean, it's difficult to keep up with all of them.
Yeah.

Different properties, and then, in terms of, like, getting them to do reviews, it's kind of… There's a certain point, it's kind of on the managers to try, because we're not… we work in the corporate office, so we're not actually, obviously, at the properties.
Right.
So it's hard… it's hard to convince… certain… certain properties are better at it, but it's hard to convince all these properties to…
Yep.
Really try to get those reviews.
Yeah, okay, that makes sense.

Yeah, yeah.
Okay, perfect.

Well, the only reason why I asked is just out of curiosity, like.
Basically, all of our tools, the way that they are automated is via integration with your property management software.
Okay.
We work with all the major players, so I imagine, you know, if y'all are looking beyond RealPage one site, which, you know, that's even kind of a bigger one.

I'm not worried, so… With that being said, we have a full product suite these days that's really focused on optimizing and standardizing your brand across the web, while also automating as much as possible, because, yeah, there's simply not enough time in the day, and yeah, when you multiply it across… I mean, even 10, 20 properties, but y'all have 200, it's just impossible.
So… The way that our tool works is, Google still really dominates search when it comes to, prospective renters who are looking for their new apartment.
So, with that being said, 87% of renters are starting their search for a new apartment on Google, and when they come across your profiles.
We, of course, want to make sure that your, again, your brand is standardized, you look as good as possible.

all of those residents are able to grab as much information just from looking at the profile, but what we do not only increases digital curb appeal, but then it also increases that SEO, because Google rewards relevant, frequent, and unique content.
to your profiles, with a little lift in SEO, a little lift on Google Maps, so on and so forth.
So… The reason why we created this tool, to really, you know, automate as much as possible is we hear more and more about diminishing manpower, in regards to marketing teams nationwide.
It seems that y'all are being asked for stronger and stronger results with less resources.

On-site teams, I know that we kind of chatted about this with the review pieces, like, on-site teams are overburdened as well, so sometimes when some of these marketing tasks fall into their wheelhouse, they're not able to get to them.
And then, ultimately, the renter experience is not standardized.
So, the way that our tool works is we integrate with, one site to do 4 things on your Google business profiles daily.
So.

the available floor plans.

And then from there.

a dog washing station, or, like, an EV charger.

special.

I don't think so.

Alright, so the last piece of our Google Business Profile Automation tool, if maps will load for me, okay… So, what we saw here, with the Q&A feed, was there were ample questions that we would see on multifamily Google business profiles that would either never get answered, or they'd get answered years later, because again, like, there's so much on everyone's plates, and it's impossible to keep up with all of this.
So what we've done to get ahead of a prospective resident even needing to ask the question is we place up to 20 questions on the profile based on, you know, of course, what your amenities are.
If y'all don't have an EV charger, like, we're not going to highlight that.
But we want to basically… place and answer the most commonly asked questions that we saw on those profiles.
So, office hours, availability, fitness center, those sort of amenities, pet policy, we basically, answer the question daily just by editing it.
Even if the answer doesn't change, for example, like, it's not likely they're gonna have I've covered parking one day and then not the next.

In editing this answer daily, we are signaling to Google that you're a super active business, therefore we can take advantage of those things that Google rewards, like the SEO increase, so on, so forth.
Any questions on that?
No, that makes sense.
Okay, well then, I believe that… concludes my Google Business Profile spiel, so we can move over into reputation management.
All right.

There are kind of several pieces to this.
It's a pretty robust reputation management platform.
But the first piece of it is consolidating those reviews.
So, I know that you've kind of touched on, like, chasing down those reviews in the first place, like, that is impossible, again, across multiple profiles.
Or properties, I mean.
So, the first piece of this tool is we are going to consolidate reviews across 200 sites and pull them in, notify you in real time when a resident has left a review.
So, we notify you, that the review has been left, and then you can actually respond within the dashboard.
So you don't need to log into Google, you don't need to do anything else, you can respond right then and there on the dashboard.

there is an AI review response recommendation if you're anything like me, and can stare at an email for far too long before I can decide what I want to… like, even if it's not a difficult email, I'm just like, yeah.
So anyway, that's one piece.
You can also set up automated responses.
So, like, for 4- and star… 5-star reviews, like, even 3-star reviews, we have partners who will just set up automated responses.
The way that those work is, we help you create preloaded templates on the front end.
And then as the review comes in, there's a little bit of a delay, so it doesn't look so automated.
And then basically we'll just cycle through those feedback response templates so it's not, like, the same response every time.
So yes, that is another piece of, again, just automating as much as possible on this.
Okay.
Any questions on that?
Will you guys ever report?
Yeah.
Reviews?
Yeah, so, that is the first piece of it, but as far as, like, review reporting, there's ample reporting that you can pull from this dashboard, all sorts of fields.
You can set up those, like, emails to be, like, sent to you automatically with how you are trending at that star rating.
So you can have those sent to you, I mean, daily, if you wanted to do that to yourself, once a week, once a month, what have you.
And it gives you a really great look at how you're performing at the property level, at the portfolio level.
You can look at it In all different… different formats, if you will.
Okay.

a few different ways that we can help here.

review this on Google.
